subject: Las Positas - Catalog - Work Experience Units and Hours Not Displaying - UAT
description:
### Work Experience Units and Hours Not Displaying

- **Module:** Catalog
- **Description:** Work experience courses are not displaying correct units and hours.
- **Expected Outcome:** Units (1–10) and work experience hours should display similarly to lecture and lab hours.
- **Example:** Should display like “54–540 hours work experience" at the end of the description like the lectures shows on other courses.
- **Deployment:** Sandbox first for formatting review.
 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/OnnpxzQeehO78DgKBXUe7rNmu/?name=image.png)
 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/01ZIvLtFNaFDHnK7PUX63eeVu/?name=image.png)

subject: Legacy Methods of Instruction
description:
For the Methods of Instruction section of our Course Outline and Admin Outline reports, legacy data is showing up for some courses.

In V2, we could select the same method on the Methods of Instruction page more than once. I think when this went over to META some of the methods that were selected more than once disappeared from the page but still show up on the reports.

It would be great if you can please make sure that only the information selected and written on the Methods of Evaluation page show up on the reports.

A specific example is attached.

comments.body:
For the Methods of Instruction section of our Course Outline and Admin Outline reports, legacy data is showing up for some courses.

In V2, we could select the same method on the Methods of Instruction page more than once. I think when this went over to META some of the methods that were selected more than once disappeared from the page but still show up on the reports.

It would be great if you can please make sure that only the information selected and written on the Methods of Evaluation page show up on the reports.

A specific example is attached.

Craig,

A possible solution, we can combine those course duplicate MOI type records into one MOI type record, then remove the extra records, this would fix your report display to no longer display duplicate MOI types, but then the users would need to update that data moving forward or if an admin user would need to go fix that combined text in the active records, if needed.

Does this work for you?

A workaround is when you come across one of these, is to go into the MOI and uncheck the duplicate MOI type, re-fresh browser, and then the other duplicate MOI type will show as checked, uncheck it, refresh browser, then it should no longer be checked, check it and fill in as needed. If there is 2 or more records for a MOI type, you will have to uncheck, refresh browser that many times to get rid of them all.


Michael Gregston
Support Supervisor

O: 208-522-1225
E: mike.gregston@acadea.com | W: www.acadea.com
A: 1600 John Adams Pkwy, Suite 200, Idaho Falls, Idaho 83401

subject: Activity Units/Hours
description:
Hello,

We have decided to use Activity Units for credit courses and Activity Hours for noncredit courses. The attached PDFs show where we would like the Activity field to be placed on the Units/Hours page, where the Activity content field would show up on the Course Content page, and where we would like Activity related stuff to show up on the Course Outline and Admin Outlines.

For the Credit courses: Activity Units should act the same way as the other unit fields. Variable units being available when the variable units checkbox is checked. Also, the units should only be allowed to be multiples of 0.5 like for other fields. The Total Activity Hours in the Calculations field should calculate at 36 hours per unit. It should add 36 hours per unit to Total Inside of Class Hours and 18 hours per unit to Total Outside of Class Hours. I believe that Total Student Learning Hours is the sum of Total Inside of Class Hours + Total Outside of Class Hours already.

For noncredit courses: all of the fields and locations should be the same as for credit courses, except the Min/Max will be in terms of Hours. There are different checkboxes for lecture, lab, and work experience on the Units/Hours page when CB04 is noncredit, so it should be different for Activity Hours as well. For the Calculations field, Total Activity Hours is the number of hours placed in the Activity Hours field and the same for the Total Inside of Class Hours. The number of Activity Hours used towards Total Outside of Class Hours is half the number of hours in the Activity Hours field. Only if the number of Outside of Class Hours and Total Student Learning Hours results in a decimal being used should the totals show the first decimal place (will only be 5 in that place ex. 1.5, 2.5, etc.). Otherwise, no decimal place.

Craig,

Just to confirm what all is being requested in this support ticket, see the below understanding. Also, please answer any questions in red text below.


- Update all course form versions.
- Page "**Units/Hours**":
  - Section "**Instructional Categories (check all that apply)**", note there is one for credit courses, and one for non-credit courses:
    - Add a new check-box field, "**Activity**", between check-boxes "**Lecture**" and "**Lab**" for both credit and non-credit course sections.
    - When the new check-box field "**Activity**" is checked, add/show new text-box field "**Min Units**" for credit course section, and "**Min Hours**" for non-credit course section, just below this new check-box. Should function like the other fields around this new field.
    - When the new check-box field "**Activity**" is checked, and "**Select here if this course will have variable units**" is "**Yes**", add/show new text-box field "**Max Units**" for credit course section, and "**Max Hours**" for non-credit course section, just below this new text-box field for min. Should function like the other fields around this new field.
    - Note, the units should only be allowed to be multiples of 0.5 like for other fields.
  - Section "**Totals**":
    - Custom display "**Calculations**":
      - Below the display "**Total Lecture Hours**", add the display "**Total Activity Hours**" for when the new check-box field "**Activity**" above is checked.
      - The "**Total Activity Hours**" in the custom display "**Calculations**" field should calculate at 36 hours per unit.
      - The "**Total Activity Hours**" should add **36 hours per unit** to "**Total Inside of Class Hours**" and **18 hours per unit** to "**Total Outside of Class Hours**".
      - Client believes the "**Total Student Learning Hours**" is the sum of "**Total Inside of Class Hours + Total Outside of Class Hours**" already.
      - "**Total Activity Hours**" is the number of hours placed in the **Activity Hours** field and the same for the **Total Inside of Class Hours**. The number of **Activity Hours** used towards **Total Outside of Class Hours** is half the number of hours in the **Activity Hours** field. Only if the number of **Outside of Class Hours** and **Total Student Learning Hours** results in a decimal being used should the totals show the first decimal place (will only be 5 in that place ex. 1.5, 2.5, etc.). Otherwise, no decimal place.
    - Note, **Activity units** should be part of the sum for the **total units**.
- Page "**Course Content**":
  - Between the RTE text-area fields "**Lecture Content**" and "**Lab content**":
    - Add the new RTE text-area field "**Activity Content**".
    - Make it **Required** when shown.
    - Should have show/hide configuration to only show when the new check-box field "**Activity**" is checked. Should function like the other fields around this new field.
- Reports "**Admin Outline**" & "**Course Outline**":
  - Credit courses:
    - After the display of the course description, add the display of "**Units Activity**" and the "**X**" value entered for that field between the display "**Units Lecture**" and "**Units Lab**".
    - When more than one unit type display, probably should be separated by a comma for sentence format, and there should be a period at the end of that custom sentence display.
  - Non-credit courses:
    - Does not seem to have this custom unit/hour type display after the course description, so leave non-credit courses as is.
  - The total calculations display should be the same for credit courses and non-credit courses as in their course form for the custom calculation display on the "**Units/Hours**" page.
  - Section "**Course Content**":
    - Between "**Lecture**" and "**Lab Activities**", add the display of the new field "**Activity Content**" as "**Activities:**".
    - If a course is only using Activity Units or Hours, then there is no title (other than Course Content above). Should function like the other fields displays around this new display.
  - "**X Units**" in the top right of report, **activity units** should be part of the sum for the **total units**.
- Catalog hour display after course description:
  - Credit courses:
    - After the display of the course description, add the display "**X**" value and "**hours activity**" between the display "**hours lecture**" and "**hours lab**" as comma separated list.
  - Non-credit courses:
    - Does not seem to have this custom type display after the course description, so leave non-credit courses as is.
  - "**X units**" in the top of course description display, **activity units** should be part of the sum for the **total units**.

Anything between GE area "**Cal-GETC**" and GE area "**Las Positas College GE**" is in the same group ordered list section and would function the same as that section configuration.

The issue with the Approved check-box not showing when
GEE is checked on page load, is a known bug, and we do have a development support ticket in to get it fixed. This item issue relates to ticket #44172.

The show/hide configuration should still be working for fields outside this group ordered list section.

Client is wanting a new GE area called "**Language Other Than English**", and then under that new GE area have the GEE check-box option "**LOTE**", and then under that have a new "**Approved**" check-box.

If this is configured outside of the Group Ordered List, the new fields will not render in any locations where the GE group checklist is currently displayed. This would introduce additional administrative and development overhead to surface those fields anywhere GE data is displayed. In some contexts, this may not be feasible or supported, depending on the configuration and display location, as certain views may not allow data merging. If the client later requests consolidated or merged data views, this approach would require additional ongoing maintenance and increased effort to support.

subject: No Unit Value Lab
description:
Hello,

On the Units/Hours page, please remove the No Unit Value Lab field. This field when checked automatically added 18 Lab Hours (and inside of class hours) to the Calculations and opened up the Lab field on the Content page.

This would only be for any new proposals (Course Modification, New Course, etc.). It would be nice that for a Course Modification of a course that currently contains the No Unit Value Lab field, if the field and content info was visible but in red and so if a faculty member unclicked the checkbox for No Unit Value Lab, and then refreshed the page, the field would no longer appear (like what you have done for the GE section where old data is in red and once unchecked it goes away).

comments.body:
Hello,

On the Units/Hours page, please remove the No Unit Value Lab field. This field when checked automatically added 18 Lab Hours (and inside of class hours) to the Calculations and opened up the Lab field on the Content page.

This would only be for any new proposals (Course Modification, New Course, etc.). It would be nice that for a Course Modification of a course that currently contains the No Unit Value Lab field, if the field and content info was visible but in red and so if a faculty member unclicked the checkbox for No Unit Value Lab, and then refreshed the page, the field would no longer appear (like what you have done for the GE section where old data is in red and once unchecked it goes away).

Craig,

This stand alone check-box field "**No Unit Value Lab**" on page "**Units/Hours**" doesn't have the same configuration/functionality as GE, so we can't apply the same configuration/functionality to this field.

Just to confirm what all is being requested in this support ticket, see the below understanding.


- Version all current active course forms and only make changes to the new form versions.
- Page "**Units/Hours**":
  - Field "**No Unit Value Lab**":
    - Remove this field.
    - When this field is checked, it calculates "**18**" in section "**Totals**", display "**Calculations**", for the following:
      - **Total Lab Hours**
      - **Total Inside of Class Hours**
      - **Total Student Learning Hours**
    - ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/dqNP77dBBSCNI1mv9CS3nmDcP/?name=image.png)
    - These three calculations, above, should no longer populate based off that check-box field being removed.
    - Make sure this check-box field is not doing anything else in any other calculated field, if it is, we will need to report back to client what it does, and find out how to update the configuration to no longer use that field in their calculations.
- Page "**Course Content**":
  - Field "**Lab Content**":
    - This field show/hide configuration should no longer look at the field being removed above.
    - If any other show/hide configuration is set up for this field that doesn't deal with the field being removed, should stay as is.
    - ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/dXinbzXlN4OYPStaljzERR0Rd/?name=image.png)
- Reports "**Admin Outline**" & "**Course Outline**":
  - The changes above need to be applied here as well.
  - Note, these three displays need to show for all existing courses, and then not show for courses created from then on that is used by the new course form versions.
  - ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/TRUVYfrUzrSVIvN0EtVZeiEnx/?name=image.png)

Does the above sound and look correct in what all is being requested?

Did I miss anything that is being requested that I did not state in the above? If it's not listed above, it won't get updated.

subject: Program Report Bug
description:
Hello!

It seems that the Program Pathway report is actually showing our Admin Narrative report information and our Admin Narrative report is showing our Program Pathway information. Please fix.

Also, for the table showing the Program Mapper information (it is on both the Program Pathway and Admin Narrative reports), when the selection on the Program Mapper page is a Non-course Requirement, the columns in the table do not line up correctly. The units are showing up below the text, and the MAJ/GEN/ELEC information is showing up in the second column instead. Please fix.

comments.body:
Hello!

It seems that the Program Pathway report is actually showing our Admin Narrative report information and our Admin Narrative report is showing our Program Pathway information. Please fix.

Also, for the table showing the Program Mapper information (it is on both the Program Pathway and Admin Narrative reports), when the selection on the Program Mapper page is a Non-course Requirement, the columns in the table do not line up correctly. The units are showing up below the text, and the MAJ/GEN/ELEC information is showing up in the second column instead. Please fix.

Craig,

Do you have some examples that you're referring to that you can give us, so we can see what you're seeing.

The "**Program Requirements**" page shows in the section "**3. Program Requirements**" in report "**Program Pathway**". See image example below.

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/t4xJoYhE1Ofkj2HT9O6D02O2t/?name=image.png)

Is the above not correct behavior? Should the "**Program Requirements**" not be in report "**Program Pathway**", and should it be in report "**Admin Narrative**"?

The "**Program Mapper**" page shows in the bottom section in report "**Program Pathway**" and shows in the report "**Admin Narrative**". See image example below.

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/nRImdiQCy3qst7R4baGzF7heS/?name=image.png)

Is the above not correct behavior? Should the "**Program Mapper**" stay in report "**Program Pathway**", and should not be in report "**Admin Narrative**"?

For the "**Non-Course Requirement**" record displaying on the "**Progam Pathway**" report and "**Admin Narrative**" report, I see "**Spring,Summer**" is displaying in the column "**Units**", and I believe you're saying it should display in column "**Semester(s) Offered**", correct? See image example below.

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/7MUScr0utuBrESCEOVMgnKVGf/?name=image.png)


Michael Gregston
Support Supervisor

O: 208-522-1225

A: 1600 John Adams Pkwy, Suite 200, Idaho Falls, Idaho 83401

subject: Skills Analysis on Admin Outline Report
description:
Hello,

For the Skills Analysis section on the Admin Outline Report, please categorize the output based on the selection of Required, Recommended, and Not Necessary. So, Required skills are listed first under "Required Skills", then Recommended skills are listed under "Recommended Skills", and then "Not Necessary skills are listed under "Not Necessary Skills".

Please do this for all Courses.

comments.body:
Hello,

For the Skills Analysis section on the Admin Outline Report, please categorize the output based on the selection of Required, Recommended, and Not Necessary. So, Required skills are listed first under "Required Skills", then Recommended skills are listed under "Recommended Skills", and then "Not Necessary skills are listed under "Not Necessary Skills".

Please do this for all Courses.

Craig,

Looking at the "**Admin Outline**" report, section "**Requisite Skills**", it looks like it only pulls in courses with the requisite type “**Prerequisite**”, it orders by the same course requisite record order, so that it matches the course form page. It does not pull in the course objectives that have “**Not Necessary**”, it only pulls course objectives that have either “**Recommended**” or “**Required**”. It then orders by the course objective order, so that it matches the order on the course form page. This same display and order are on the “**Distance Education Addendum**” report.

To change this display, to group by the “**Degree of Importance**”, would mix the course objectives between courses. Is that what you are wanting?

Or are you saying keep them group by course, then under course group by the “**Degree of Importance**”?

Just to confirm, you are wanting course objectives that have “**Not Necessary**” to now display in the “**Admin Outline**” report, correct?

Just to confirm, you are wanting headers “**Required**”, “**Recommended**”, and “**Not Necessary**” just above each course objective group, correct?

Are you wanting the same changes done to the report “**Distance Education Addendum**”?

Do you have an example of the format you are wanting, using an existing proposal example data?

Would you rather leave the display as is and then, at the end of each course objective, just add the "**Degree of Importance**" saved per each objective in parentheses?

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/OJIEIStordpGiUxPZ0RW51gOr/?name=image.png)

If so, should this text be added every time a course is selected, or how do we know which requisite records should have this text and which ones should not?

subject: LPC- Course - Equity Based Curriculum
description:
Change Measurable Objectives to Course Objectives
Assignments to Typical Assignments
Remove Library
Typical Texts change to Textbooks/Materials
Move Catalog Descrpition to the top of this list

The instructional text -

This section defines the ways instructors will ensure curriculum is designed to promote equitable access to all students, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender, sexuality, socio-economic status, or disability.

Equity based curriculum promotes a shared level of access with open pathways that are equidistant to mutually agreed-upon currencies. All curriculum content designed including assessments must be free from bias; fair across race, religion, ethnicity and gender; and culturally relevant with the mindful integration of diverse communities, cultures, histories and contributions. This includes attention to African-American, Latin-American, Asian, indigenous people, women, LGBTQ, religious minorities (including Muslims), working class people and youth. The following list displays common areas of equity based curriculum considerations. The actual implementation of equity based curriculum will be listed in the instructor's syllabus.

For any areas selected below, include 1-2 sentences explaining how equity is addressed in that part of the Course Outline of Record
Common areas of equity based curriculum consideration may include, but are not limited to, the following

Change to

Please select which elements of the COR describe approaches faculty may use to accommodate and engage diverse student bodies, advanced equitable student outcomes, and promote the inclusion of all students, and which elements of the COR use or describe approaches faculty may use to accommodate the needs and abilities of all learners and develop a flexible learning environment in which information is presented in multiple ways, students engage in learning in a variety of ways, and students are provided options when demonstrating their learning.

subject: Las Positas - Courses - Issue with validation error
description:
Issue with validation error, "**A course with this course number and subject code already exisits.**"

Course "**AUTO L1**" (6145) display the error above, this is due to the extra course family "**AUTO L1**" (2593).

Client current workaround is to update course "**AUTO L1**" (2593) number to "**AUTO L1'**" (2593), so that they can move forward with the course (6145).


- We need to update course ID (**2593**) to be in the same course family as course ID (**6145**).
- We need to remove the apostrophe from the course number from course ID (**2539**) and make sure the course ID (**6145**) does not throw that validation error when the course is in draft status.
- We also need to fix the spelling for the word "**exisits**" to "**exists**" in the custom validation error.

subject: Special Report
description:
Hello!

I am hoping you can create a special report that shows Effective Term of the most recent full update (Course Modification or New Course) of a course proposal. A course should only show up on the list if there is an Active version of a proposal of any type. Again, it would be for all Active courses, we need the course Effective Term of the most recent version of which ever of the two proposal types "Course Modification" or "New Course" is newest, even if that version of the proposal is Historical.

comments.body:
Hello!

I am hoping you can create a special report that shows Effective Term of the most recent full update (Course Modification or New Course) of a course proposal. A course should only show up on the list if there is an Active version of a proposal of any type. Again, it would be for all Active courses, we need the course Effective Term of the most recent version of which ever of the two proposal types "Course Modification" or "New Course" is newest, even if that version of the proposal is Historical.

Craig,

I went to your production site under Reports > Saved AdHoc Reports, and created the new ad-hoc report "**Active Courses Effective Term**".

This report pulls courses that have not been deleted and where courses have the status of **Active**.

Please take a look and let us know if this report is will work for what you're wanting and if this help completes this ticket, or if more changes are need, let us know.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike,

Sorry, but this is not what I am hoping for. 

For any course that is Active I want the date of latest version of the proposal types Course Modification or New Course, whichever they have that is newest. This may include active proposals of these types, or historical if the active proposal type is not a Course Modification or New Course.

Craig,

To confirm what you're wanting, an example of what I believe you're saying, if you have a course family with the following statuses:

- Historical > Active > Approved > Lunched > Draft
  - You want to see the Draft version.
- Historical > Active > Approved > Lunched
  - You want to see the Launched version.
- Historical > Active > Approved
  - You want to see the Approved version.
- Historical > Active
  - You want to see the Active version.
- Historical > Historical
  - You want to see the Historical version.

Does the above sound correct?

Then pull the same data fields that I pulled in that ad-hoc report I created on the production site, correct?


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike,

As far as an algorithm:

1. Active course proposal?
    If no, ignore that course and move to the next one.
    If yes, step 2.
2. Is Active course proposal type a Course Modification or New Course?
    If yes, use the effective term of that proposal.
    If no, use the effective term of the most recent Historical Course Modification or New    Course (whichever is most recent)?

Craig,

Lets have a call and go over this request. When is a good day/time for you?

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Thursday after 2 pm works for me.

subject: General Education/Transfer Request page changes
description:
Hello!

I am hoping to get some changes to nearly all elements of the General Education/Transfer Request page.

1. For all elements, when selected there should be a Comments text box that opens up (does not require text). Currently this only happens for when "New Request" is selected, but I would rather it happens for any request. For example, when I click on CSU Transfer, then Transfers to a CSU, the text box should show up. Same if I select Cal-GETC, then one of the areas, the textbox should open up. C-ID Proposal is the only area that shouldn't change how it currently works, except for what I ask for in #2.
2. Please remove things that say "New Request", "Already Approved", "Already approved substantial change", and "Already approved unsubstantial change". Please add one check box element that says "Approved". It would be nice if only Admin can check or uncheck the "Approved" box.
3. Please remove "Other articulation requests/comments"

comments.body:
Hello!

I am hoping to get some changes to nearly all elements of the General Education/Transfer Request page.

1. For all elements, when selected there should be a Comments text box that opens up (does not require text). Currently this only happens for when "New Request" is selected, but I would rather it happens for any request. For example, when I click on CSU Transfer, then Transfers to a CSU, the text box should show up. Same if I select Cal-GETC, then one of the areas, the textbox should open up. C-ID Proposal is the only area that shouldn't change how it currently works, except for what I ask for in #2.
2. Please remove things that say "New Request", "Already Approved", "Already approved substantial change", and "Already approved unsubstantial change". Please add one check box element that says "Approved". It would be nice if only Admin can check or uncheck the "Approved" box.
3. Please remove "Other articulation requests/comments"

Craig,

Currently, your GE page looks like this:

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/yCp7OT3WedYWTtyO80W8Qe0XS/?name=image.png)

And we would like to update it to pull all the GE/GEE general education/general education element data into one group section, so it would look something like this (_note, but with your data_), we are trying to uniform the GE data across all clients, so that it will function more user-friendly as needed. The GE options would no longer be check-boxes and would be group headers, and then only the GEE options would be check-boxes (_less clicking/checking a check-box_). This will ensure in the future updates are seamless and can be put in your own hands so it can all be done without having to request a support ticket.

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/EKE3QO4VoOYcJvcuUp4CpCyGd/?name=image.png)

Would you be okay with that to help clean up this page view?

For your original requests for this ticket:

1. Yes, we can add a "**Comments**" text-area to display for each GEE - general education element that is checked.
2. Yes, we can remove the following check-box fields "**New Request**", "**Already Approved**", "**Already approved substantial change**", and "**Already approved unsubstantial change**", where exists in this GE page:
  1. We can also add one check-box, "**Approved**" per each GEE checked option.
  2. Should the text-area only show for the GEE being checked, or should another one also show for "**Approved**" being checked? I would think you only need the one text-area per GEE, and then leave the check-box by itself per each GEE.
  3. We can look into if we can add role admin permissions for the "**Approved**" check-box to just an admin user being able to edit and role user to only be able to view.
3. Yes, we can remove the check-box "**Other articulation requests/comments**" and also remove the text-area that goes with it.

We would move the field  "**C-ID proposal**" out of the middle of this GE/GEE section to the bottom of the page, where this field should only be the one check-box and the one text-box that goes with it.

Are there any existing course reports that would need updated based off the updates being requested above, if so, which ones, and what formatting are you wanting changed?


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225
This ticket relates to ticket #43339.

Hi Mike,

Here are the changes that have been made regarding this ticket.


- Updated the course “**General Education/Transfer Request**” page, by converting the current configuration over to the one group checklist for GE/GEE.
- When a GEE is checked, added a "**Comments**" text-area (show/hide configuration), this should appear for each GEE - general education element that is checked.
- Removed all the following check-box fields "**New Request**", "**Already Approved**", "**Already approved substantial change**", and "**Already approved unsubstantial change**", where exists in this GE page:
  1. Added one check-box, "**Approved**" to display per each GEE checked option
    1. Add role **Admin** permissions **Edit** for the "**Approved**" check-box field above and add role **User** permission **View**.
    2. Only admin users should be able to edit this check-box field, and then non-admin users can view.
- Removed the check-box "**Other articulation requests/comments**" and also remove the text-area that goes with it.
- Moved the field  "**C-ID proposal**" out of the middle of this GE/GEE section to the bottom of the page, where this field should only be the one check-box and the one text-box that goes with it.
- If all GEE areas are deactivated, then no longer show the GE group header, but be sure to show the saved (GE/GEE) when exists for historical purposes.
- The CSU GE or IGETC stuff don’t need to show up anymore, so those can disappear, unless there is saved data for historical purposes, make it display in red text, so that the user can uncheck in modification as needed.
- Report “**Admin Outline**”:
  - Added the information from the course “**General Education/Transfer Request**” page, to display the new one group OL checklist, and show the group header (GE), then show the (GEE) checked areas just above the “**Codes and Dates**” section.
  - Made sure this new section on the report uses the same header format as the other page sections in this report.
  - If data inputted into the text-area “**Comments**” display on the report.
  - If “**Approved**” is checked, display on the report.
  - If all GEE areas are deactivated, then no longer show the GE group header, but be sure to show the saved (GE/GEE) when exists for historical purposes.
- Made sure, after adding the GE/GEE info to the “**Admin Outline**” report, it also displays in the “**Admin Outline Comparison**” report.

subject: Export - fix needed
description:
Hello,

We we conduct a search for Courses or Programs, and then Export the data, we do not get data that we requested. The exported data does show the Courses or Programs that show up in the Search, but the columns do not match with the Output Fields.

Please fix the Export data so that each column corresponds to a select in the selected Output Fields for any given search.

subject: Outside of Class Hours for Noncredit Lecture Hours - UAT
description:
Hello,

For noncredit course outline reports, I would like the section that currently shows "Total Noncredit Hours" to mirror how hours is shown for credit courses.
Total Lecture Hours
Total Laboratory Hours
Total Inside of Class Hours
Total Outside of Class Hours
Total Noncredit Hours

They should be calculated the same way as for a credit course: Total Outside of Class Hours is double the number of Lecture Hours. Total Inside of Class Hours is Total Lecture Hours + Total Lab Hours.

These changes should show up on the Course Outline report and Admin Outline report.

Thanks!

comments.body:

For noncredit course outline reports, I would like the section that currently shows "Total Noncredit Hours" to mirror how hours is shown for credit courses.
Total Lecture Hours
Total Laboratory Hours
Total Inside of Class Hours
Total Outside of Class Hours
Total Noncredit Hours

They should be calculated the same way as for a credit course: Total Outside of Class Hours is double the number of Lecture Hours. Total Inside of Class Hours is Total Lecture Hours + Total Lab Hours.

Thanks!
Also, Total Noncredit Hours is Total Inside of Class Hours + Total Outside of Class Hours

subject: Admin Outline report for Courses
description:
Hello,

I am hoping for a couple of changes to the Admin Outline report for courses. I would like it to show only the Course Outline report, but if Yes is selected on the Distance Education page, the Distance Education Addendum report is attached, and if Yes is selected on the Credit for Prior Learning page, the CPL Request report is attached. All of this as a single pdf is preferrable.

comments.body:
Hello,

I am hoping for a couple of changes to the Admin Outline report for courses. I would like it to show only the Course Outline report, but if Yes is selected on the Distance Education page, the Distance Education Addendum report is attached, and if Yes is selected on the Credit for Prior Learning page, the CPL Request report is attached. All of this as a single pdf is preferrable.

Craig,

As far as I know of, we don't have a way to display three different reports into one report.

We might be able to add hyperlinks at the bottom of the first report that points to those other reports based off those fields' selection.

Or we can add those field displays in those other two reports to the first report and have show/hide those fields based off those fields' selection, but note the headers from those other reports wouldn't display in this one report, just the fields from them.

Or we can create try to create a completely brand-new custom report, and you tell us what all you want in this brand-new report.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

The current Admin Outline report is custom but is solely a pdf report - no html. Is adjusting that not possible? Just want to basically have it combine the pdf versions of the reports requested in the ticket.

comments.body:
Hello,

For some reason I can add things on the Measurable Objectives page, but I cannot remove anything for the launched MUS 8A course modification. Please fix ASAP.

Hi Craig

The reason that these Objectives can not be removed is because MUS 8A is a requisite of other courses, and on those other courses they are doing a skill Analysis/Requisiste Analysis that map the objectives to the course they are a requisite too. To remove a Objective first one needs to remove all the Objective mapping to the Courses it is a Requisite to on their Requisites/Requisite Validation tab. Let me know if that makes sence or if you need any help figuring that out.

Thanks,
Justin Palmer

Craig,

Looking at V2 on program page "**Narrative**" for field "**Similar Programs at Other Colleges in Service Area**", it looks like it had show/hide logic to only show:

- If the program does NOT have one of the award types:
  - **Certificate of Completion (Noncredit CDCP)**
  - **Certificate of Competency (Noncredit CDCP)**
- Then display this field for user input, but only save the data inputted in this field if the program has one of the following award types:
  - **A.S. - Associates in Science Degree**
  - **A.A. - Associates in Arts Degree**
  - **B CA - Certificate of Achievement (12 to fewer than 18 semester units)**
  - **A CA - Certificate of Achievement (18 or greater semester units)**
Are you wanting to keep this show/hide logic, or do you want to change the show/hide logic for this field in Meta to only show when program award type is "**CTE (all non-ADT awards with CTE TOP-Codes)**"?

If you go with the second option, should there be any other show/hide logic added to this field?

If this field is shown, do you want it to be required?

Just to confirm, this new field should be item 8 on the report "**Program Narrative**" correct?

subject: New Local GE Pattern
description:
Hello,

For the General Education/Transfer page:

1. Please remove Effective Semester fields for all of the options.
2. For C-ID proposal, please add the New Request, Already approved substantial change, and already approved unsubstantial change fields to it like for the others, and only an Admin or Articulation Officer can amend them (whatever limitation is on the others).
3. Please hide the text boxes unless New Request is selected for a GE or transfer element. 
3. Please completely hide the IGETC and CSU GE check boxes and information (they are currently grayed out) for course modifications, technical course modifications, and even the special workflows (SLO, DE Addendum, CPL).
4. Please replace Las Positas College GE with the following:

1A - English Composition
1B - Oral Communication and Critical Thinking
2 - Mathematical Concepts and Quantitative Reasoning
3 - Arts and Humanities
4 - Social and Behavioral Sciences
5 - Natural Sciences
6 - Ethnic Studies
7 - Kinesiology
8 - Health
9 - American Institutions

I think you probably need to create new GeneralEducationId. I see in the General Education Element page under Lookup Manager, that the GeneralEducationId say Chabot College GE. These can go inactive and be hidden for any updates, and new GeneralEducationId can be made titled Las Positas College GE. They only need the New Request and Already Approved fields. Text box only shows if New Request is selected. No need to transfer over the data from the current GE fields.

comments.body:
Hello,

For the General Education/Transfer page:

1. Please remove Effective Semester fields for all of the options.
2. For C-ID proposal, please add the New Request, Already approved substantial change, and already approved unsubstantial change fields to it like for the others, and only an Admin or Articulation Officer can amend them (whatever limitation is on the others).
3. Please hide the text boxes unless New Request is selected for a GE or transfer element. 
3. Please completely hide the IGETC and CSU GE check boxes and information (they are currently grayed out) for course modifications, technical course modifications, and even the special workflows (SLO, DE Addendum, CPL).
4. Please replace Las Positas College GE with the following:

1A - English Composition
1B - Oral Communication and Critical Thinking
2 - Mathematical Concepts and Quantitative Reasoning
3 - Arts and Humanities
4 - Social and Behavioral Sciences
5 - Natural Sciences
6 - Ethnic Studies
7 - Kinesiology
8 - Health
9 - American Institutions

I think you probably need to create new GeneralEducationId. I see in the General Education Element page under Lookup Manager, that the GeneralEducationId say Chabot College GE. These can go inactive and be hidden for any updates, and new GeneralEducationId can be made titled Las Positas College GE. They only need the New Request and Already Approved fields. Text box only shows if New Request is selected. No need to transfer over the data from the current GE fields.

subject: Requisite text required for common course numbers
description:
Hello,

I know that we can now Launch a course that has a requisite course that is in Active, Approved, Draft, or Launched status. We also need to be able to launch a course if a Prerequisite or Recommended Course Preparation is selected, but no course is selected - only text is added to the comments section that shows up on the course outline like it does now. This is required for our Common Course Number outlines. Can you please make it so only text needs to be in the comments section of a requisite if no course is selected? This is different than an Enrollment Limitation, as we need the course outline to specifically say prerequisite for a couple of our CCNs this round.

This is different than an Enrollment Limitation, as we need the course outline to specifically say prerequisite for a couple of our CCNs this round.
Please see STAT C1000 as an example of what we need to be able to launch.

subject: Transfer Degree Catalog Language
description:
Hello,

Currently on the Narrative page for all Associate in Science for Transfer degrees and Associate in Arts for Transfer degrees, there is a section of text under the Catalog Description box that starts with "Completion Requirements".

Please change this text to:

Completion Requirements: 1. Completion of 60 semester units or 90 quarter units that are eligible for transfer to the California State University, including both of the following: a. The California Intersegmental General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC). b. A minimum of 18 semester units in a major or area of emphasis, as determined by the community college district. 2. Obtainment of a minimum grade point average of 2.0. Associate Degrees for Transfer (ADT''s) also require that students must earn a "C" (or "P") or better in all courses required for the major or area of emphasis.

This should also show up as part of the Catalog Description on both the Program Narrative report and the Admin Narrative report (still being worked on).

subject: Special Proposal Effective Terms
description:
Hello,

For the SLO, PSLO, CPL, DE, and Program Mapper proposal types, I would like the Effective Term on the Cover page to be the same Effective Term as the current Active/Approved version of the course/program. The Effective Term for these proposal types will be recorded on their individual pages, but since they are not updates to Title 5 requirements, the official Effective Term of the Course Outline, program Narrative, and Program Requirements should not change. Please fix ASAP. 

Also, there are several AJ courses in the workflow with a CPL proposal type with no Effective Term on the cover page. Please make it the same Effective Term as the current Active versions of the courses.

comments.body:
Hello,

For the SLO, PSLO, CPL, DE, and Program Mapper proposal types, I would like the Effective Term on the Cover page to be the same Effective Term as the current Active/Approved version of the course/program. The Effective Term for these proposal types will be recorded on their individual pages, but since they are not updates to Title 5 requirements, the official Effective Term of the Course Outline, program Narrative, and Program Requirements should not change. Also, there are several AJ courses in the workflow with a CPL proposal type with no Effective Term on the cover page. Please make it the same Effective Term as the current Active versions of the courses.

Craig,

Why is the filed "**Effective Term**" on page "**Cover**" read only on the form "**Credit for Prior Learning**" that uses the proposal type "**Credit for Prior Learning**"?

Example, course **AJ 50** (5620) that is using the proposal type/form above.

Any time a modification course is made, this field should be editable for the originator to be able to select as needed.

Do you want me to make this field editable?

Note, if we do make this field editable, the originator can only edit it if the course is in **Draft** status. Where this course is in **Launched** status, an admin user would have to update the field for the originator, or if action request changes in workflow is taken, then the originator can make the change as needed.

For now, I can update the **Launched AJ** courses to have the same term as their parent proposal if you want. Then, if we make the field "**Effective Term**" editable to originator, the originator can go update the **Draft** courses as needed. Currently, I see five **AJ** courses that have **Launched** status.

I'm not seeing any courses that use the following proposal types that do not have an effective term:

- **Distance Education**
- **Student Learning Outcomes (SLOs)**
Do you have any examples I can go off of which courses that use one of these two proposal types that need their "**Effective Term**" updated, and what they need updated too?

I do not see any programs currently using the proposal type "**Program Student Learning Outcomes (PSLOs)**". Do you have an example I can look at?

Looks like the filed "**Effective Term**" on page "**Cover**" for pograms is also read only on the form "**Program Student Learning Outcomes (PSLOs)**" that uses the proposal type "**Program Student Learning Outcomes (PSLOs)**"?

Do you want us to make that field editable to the originator as well?

Regards,

These special proposals are not changes to catalog information. The only proposals that need the effective term editable are new, modified, and technical course and program proposals. We also do need it for the PSLOs workflow.

Yes, please change the AJ CPL requests to the effective term of their current active version. No other special proposal types have been submitted yet.

(maybe the data was changed since I first ran the report - but we need to look and make sure that something is not updating in the background and changing the value int he report.

Please do this for both the Course Outline and Admin Report

subject: No Unit Value Lab
description:
Hello,

On the Units/Hours page, for credit courses, there should be a field called No Unit Value Lab with a check box next to it. It automatically should add 18 hours of Laboratory Hours to the course, 18 hours of Inside of Class Hours, and open up the Lab Content field on the Course Content page.

We have a number of courses with this selected in V2, so that means the data is not showing correctly in META. MATH 40 should have a No Unit Value Lab selected. In V2, we have the same check box, and then an addendum that shows up when checked. The Assignments information in the addendum should be what is placed in the Lab Content field in META.

I thought this was done previously, but I see it is missing now.

subject: Course Outline Report and PDF
description:
Hello,

I took a screenshot of a Course Outline report and attached what the pdf looks like when I go to print it. I also attached the V2 outline of the same course.

1. Not all of the data from V2 regarding Textbooks are in META. I see that Other Learning Materials is showing, but blank on the Report. There is information in the Other field in V2, but it is not showing up in META. Please fix for all courses.

2. The Report and the PDF output of the report have something called Abbreviated Class Description at the very bottom. It seems to show Requisite information. Please remove.

3. The pdf version of the Report does show the Recommended Course Preparation under the catalog description, which is correct, but that is not showing at all on the html version of the Report.

4. You can see on the V2 outline I attached, the skills analysis information shows up on the report in a section called PREREQUISITE AND/OR ADVISORY SKILLS:, and then has a sentence below it, and then A. CNT7401 and then shows some of the Measurable Objectives that were selected in the Skills Analysis for the Requisite Validation. This section should be on the Course Outline report. Any requisite that is a Prerequisite or Recommended Course Preparation should have this section in the Course Outline.
   A. Instead of PREREQUISITE AND/OR ADVISORY SKILLS, please title it REQUISITE SKILLS.
   B. Some courses have both Prerequisites and Recommended Course Preparation requisites, so all Prerequisites will go first (with their own lead sentence) and then Recommended Course Preparation is second.
   C. The sentence to lead off the Prerequisites section should be as follows: "Before entering this course, it is required that a student be able to:"
   D. The sentence to lead off the Recommended Course Preparation section should be as follows: "Before entering this course, it is recommended that a student be able to:"

comments.body:
Hello,

I took a screenshot of a Course Outline report and attached what the pdf looks like when I go to print it. I also attached the V2 outline of the same course.

1. Not all of the data from V2 regarding Textbooks are in META. I see that Other Learning Materials is showing, but blank on the Report. There is information in the Other field in V2, but it is not showing up in META. Please fix for all courses.

2. The Report and the PDF output of the report have something called Abbreviated Class Description at the very bottom. It seems to show Requisite information. Please remove.

3. The pdf version of the Report does show the Recommended Course Preparation under the catalog description, which is correct, but that is not showing at all on the html version of the Report.

4. You can see on the V2 outline I attached, the skills analysis information shows up on the report in a section called PREREQUISITE AND/OR ADVISORY SKILLS:, and then has a sentence below it, and then A. CNT7401 and then shows some of the Measurable Objectives that were selected in the Skills Analysis for the Requisite Validation. This section should be on the Course Outline report. Any requisite that is a Prerequisite or Recommended Course Preparation should have this section in the Course Outline.
   A. Instead of PREREQUISITE AND/OR ADVISORY SKILLS, please title it REQUISITE SKILLS.
   B. Some courses have both Prerequisites and Recommended Course Preparation requisites, so all Prerequisites will go first (with their own lead sentence) and then Recommended Course Preparation is second.
   C. The sentence to lead off the Prerequisites section should be as follows: "Before entering this course, it is required that a student be able to:"
   D. The sentence to lead off the Recommended Course Preparation section should be as follows: "Before entering this course, it is recommended that a student be able to:"

The client called and said the **Advanced Filters > Originator** option is not working how he wanted it to. He wanted it to work like how it did in V2, where when you select "**Course Author**" on the "**My Approvals**" page, it pulls all proposals that are waiting for originator changes.

Talking with other developers, I let the client know he can use **Advanced Filters > Pending Changes > equal > Yes**, and this will show him proposals that are waiting for originator changes.

The client said he would review using it this way, and then will let us know if he wants something different or not.

description:
Hello,

I would like the following two users in the following Position to have the specified page edit access when a proposal is at their level for review/action in a workflow:

1. Library Coordinator - they should be able to edit the Textbooks/Materials and Library pages for course proposals.
2. Counseling - they should be able to edit the Program Mapper page for program proposals.


I would also like the SLO Chair to be able to edit the Program Learning Outcomes page for program proposals and Student Learning Outcomes page for course proposals for any proposals in Launched status.

Lastly, I would like the Guided Pathways Administrator to be able to edit the Program Mapper page for any program proposals in Launched status.

subject: Admin only Program Narrative Report
description:
Hello,

Please make a Report available for Admin only, for each program that contains the following parts:

1. The first part is the Program Narrative report. If the Program Goal is Transfer, please add the Program Requirements report after the Narrative (the Program Requirements is already on the Program Narrative report for all other programs).
3. The second part is the Abridged Comparison report, if the program is a Program Modification or Technical Program Revision. If the proposal is for a New Program, please add the TOP Code and CIP code.
4. The last part is the Program Pathway report. A ticket was submitted earlier to greatly modify this report - Ticket #40352 - so, it should be the same output as that request.

Hi Justin,

What you wrote above has been done, but there are still a couple of things that need doing from the original ticket and something new I forgot was missing:

1. The Groups that were made with “OR” and “AND” look correct, but the Group should have “Exclude” selected.
2. For the Groups that are headers (ex. Required Core, List A, List B,  Additional General Education and Elective Units, etc.), “Override” is selected when it should be, but the units are not entered that were in V2. The units are just blank. I think the only place we would have 0 or no units entered is for “Total Units for the Major”, otherwise, we entered units if we were overriding the courses.
3. Something I forgot to ask is that our “Exception” information did not come over. For some programs, we had an Exception Identifier selected and an Exception entered. This information did not come over from V2.

Please take a look at the Anthropology AA-T that is active and compare it to the Active version in V2. You can see the exception identifier and unit ranges for Groups. You will notice the unit ranges on the “Additional General Education and Elective Units” Group is in reverse order. This is so the units total to 60 for the degrees.

Thanks!!

subject: Program Map
description:
Hello,

I am hoping we can make some changes to the Program Mapper page and Report.

1. For the main page, I know our current maps are showing up exactly like the Program Requirements page layout, but we don't do our maps that way (see attached example - Program Mapper Report Template.pdf). Our maps are setup term by term. Instead of the Course Block Definition being a field that titles the Term, please make it be a field we can input notes into (see attachment - Program Mapper Page.pdf) that would show up on the Program Mapper report. If we can add a field where we select the Term (1st, 2nd, 3rd, etc.) and another for selecting the semester (Fall, Spring, Summer), then those would be the title of the section (ex. "Term 1 - Fall Semester").

2. On the Edit Program Courses page: Please remove the "Term" field for when we add a Course, Grouping, or Non-Course Requirement. Please change "Term Course" to "Semester(s) Offered". Please add a field titled "Category" with a drop down with three choices that can be selected: "Major/Required", "General Education", and "Elective".

3. For the Program Mapper report:  please look at the first attachment and follow that output as closely as possible. The current one has things that are unnecessary and or missing, like "The sequence numbers it the recommended order in which courses should be taken". Not needed. I do like the layout for the program title and type, our logo. etc.

subject: Deleted Programs and Courses
description:
Hello,
When I select "Deleted" as a filter for programs or courses, I don't see anything. I assumed that any program or course that was deleted would show up with that filter on. Can that be fixed? I need to recover some programs I accidentally deleted today.

I did a search for Draft programs, and there were a number of them that showed up with no information for any field, so I deleted a bunch of them. I decided to refresh, because I didn't see any of the drafts I had made, and then  all of the real drafts showed up but I must have deleted a bunch accidentally when the information wasn't showing up. 

If there is no way to look at deleted programs, is there a way to refresh our production site for 6 pm PST? That time or earlier in the day if that time cannot be done. I am hoping to recover the deleted programs.

Thanks!

comments.body:
Hello,
When I select "Deleted" as a filter for programs or courses, I don't see anything. I assumed that any program or course that was deleted would show up with that filter on. Can that be fixed? I need to recover some programs I accidentally deleted today.

I did a search for Draft programs, and there were a number of them that showed up with no information for any field, so I deleted a bunch of them. I decided to refresh, because I didn't see any of the drafts I had made, and then  all of the real drafts showed up but I must have deleted a bunch accidentally when the information wasn't showing up. 

If there is no way to look at deleted programs, is there a way to refresh our production site for 6 pm PST? That time or earlier in the day if that time cannot be done. I am hoping to recover the deleted programs.

Thanks!

Sorry if I am not clear above. I would like to be able to make any Programs that were deleted after 6 pm PST to be put back as drafts. I would also like to be able to see deleted courses and programs. That can just be for people with admin status.

Hi Craig,

I have assigned this ticket to a developer's queue. The developer will be in contact with you if they have any questions.

Hi Craig,

I am the developer assigned to this ticket. So, currently the interface doesn't have a way to access a program that has been deleted. However, when a program or course are deleted through the interface, they actually only get deactivated, but the data is not erased. So, you can always access a deleted course/program if you directly paste its Id in the URL, which you can get from us if you no longer have access to it.

I went ahead and reactivated those drafts, so you'll be able to access them once the script is deployed to live.

Regards,

Daniel D.

Hi Daniel,

Thank you! So, if I understand you correctly, you will be reactivating the recently deleted Drafts, but I will never be able to access deleted data on my own without the URL - got it! What does the "Deleted" Status option do when searching for courses or programs?

subject: Program Requirements data corrections
description:
Hello,

The Program Requirement information came over with some information missing that I am hoping you can correct. I made corrections to the Art History AA-T that is in Launched status to show how we want the information to come over. Please compare it to the current Active version of the degree Program Requirements and both to the Active version in V2.

1. Mostly anything in a Group has the units overridden, but no units in the override. The only time the group should have an override if it was entered that way in V2. It would be better to have the default not have the group Override selected unless it was entered that way in V2.

2. Anything that is not a course, should be converted to a Non-Course Requirement instead of a Course where no course was selected. This was corrected for when text was entered in the Non-Course Requirement, but we also use it for spacing and for entering units near the bottom of degrees. In our degrees we write "Total Units for the Major" and in V2 we make sure the "override" units are 0, but they are blank in META. The section after sometimes has units that are overriden, and the units are in a reverse order so we get the total to be 60 (see Art History launched version).

3. In V2, we could select the course, and then select "or", but now that can only be done by using a Group, so all of our "or" information from V2 is missing in META. Can you bring over the information putting the courses that contain an "or" in a Group, but the Group has Exclude selected? I did this in the Launched Art History proposal.

I looked at a program proposal that was in the workflow, removed it from the workflow, and then saw the BACCC thin show up. When I looked again, a different extra field was showing - the one for Career Opportunities that should never show up for a transfer degree. This is for the Polirical Science AA-T draft.

Also, the origination date is not showing up. In V2, we could change the origination date of a proposal to move it to a date where changes had taken effect. Is that possible in META?

Hello,

I just refreshed the Narrative page, and now all of the fields show up again.

Please tie Narrative fields to Program Goal on the Cover page.

Program Goal:

1. Transfer (ADTs and Cal-GETC certificates only) - Narrative should just include "Statement of Program Goals and Objectives" and the "Catalog Description".

2. Local (Community Need) - Narrative should include everything except "Recommended by the BACCC" and "Career Opportunities". 

3. CTE (all non-ADT awards with CTE TOP-Codes) - Narrative should include every field.

4. For the remaining four options - Narrative should just include Narrative should just include "Statement of Program Goals and Objectives", the "Catalog Description", and "Master Planning".

As a quick follow-up: for programs with a goal of Transfer (ADTs and Cal-GETC certificates only), on the Narrative page there is some text after the Catalog Description. This should be showing up on the Narrative Report as part of the catalog description. I think it was showing at one point, but not anymore. It was formatted as well (see attached for an example).

Hi Mike,



My apologies, I didn't realize I can edit or delete the proposal types myself. I will change those titles.



I did finish the workflows, so I am hoping it is possible to have some of the fields in the codes and dates filled in based upon certain different approval steps:



1. CC Approval – Curriculum Committee Chair Approval in Course Proposal, Course Modification, Course Deactivation, Program Proposal, Program Modification, and Program Deactivation.
2. Board of Trustees – Board of Trustees Approval at Board of Trustees step in Course Proposal, Course Modification, Course Deactivation, Program Proposal, Program Modification, and Program Deactivation.
3. State Approval – COCI Chaptering Approval at COCI Chaptering step in Course Proposal, Course Modification, Course Deactivation, Program Proposal, Program Modification, and Program Deactivation. I did ask for State Approval to change to COCI Chaptering on an older ticket.



I have a quick question about workflow. I see when I add a position to a level, and I can select an action for the position, there are two lists – Request Change and Vote. Some actions are under both. I assume Supported moves the proposal forward, but which Supported does that? Are the Supported the same?



Reviewed under Vote should not do anything. Revisions Requested should send it back to the Originator, Requested Changes Made sends it back forward from the Originator, and Approve and Approve for Board Agenda move it forward – is all that correct? Implementation should make it Active or Historical on whatever date is entered, correct? Is there really a function difference between Implementation and Approve for Later Implementation?



Thank you!



Craig Kutil



Articulation Officer

Professor, Mathematics

Instructor, Martial Arts

Faculty at Large, Faculty Association

Las Positas College

(925) 424-1346

http://laspositascollege.edu/faculty/ckutil

http://www.laspositascollege.edu/lpcarticulation/

* * *

Craig,

The group of actions under **Request Change**, is for when that action is taken, to move the proposal back to originator to make changes.

The group of actions under **Vote**, is for when that action is taken, to move the proposal forward or backwards to other step/levels.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/KowO8Qo2kQnDKn7xupgZVCVfQ/?name=image.png)

Normally, you would only have one action under group **Request Change**. That action would be **Request Changes**. Looks like you all use the verbiage **Revisions Requested**. Doesn't matter what the name is, they both do the same thing.

The other actions under that group can be deactivated if you don't want them in the list as an option and are really not needed.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/w5rIO7DaBtbXtCVYpu1Hlqyf8/?name=image.png)

Note, you can't deactivate an action if it is being used in an existing workflow somewhere. To be able to deactivate those actions, our support team would have to do that for you. Do you want us to deactivate any actions under group **Request Changes**, or under group **Vote**, if so, which ones?

The action **Reviewed**, under group **Vote**, is normally used for **Optional** positions, like **Curriculum Committee Members**. This allows many users to have the same position, and it allows all of those users to take action **Reviewed** and add their comments if they want to, but they don't have to if they don't want too, where it is **Optional**.

You can also use action **Reviewed** under **Vote** for a **Required** step/level that only one user would take action to move forward or backwards to another step/level if you wanted.

You don't need the action **Requested Changes Made**, there is already a default action with that title (or something like that), that you don't see in the drop-down, because that action is by default there for when a proposal is moved back to the originator for changes.

It doesn't matter what the action title is, all actions under **Request Change** do the exact same thing, and the same goes for actions under **Vote**. It all does the same thing, based off how you set it up in the level properties per step/level.

Whether you take action **Implement** or action **Approved for later Implementation**, it all does the same thing, based off how you set it up in the level properties that have those actions.

No, there is no difference between actions **Implement**, or action **Approved for later Implementation**. They both do the exact same thing, but depending on what implement on date the user enters, it does change things. If you enter the current date, it makes the proposal go active right there and then. If you enter a future date, then the proposal goes to approved status, and stays approved until that implement on date comes to past, which then it will become active.

The active or historical status is based off what you select for that action, and if you have the **Completes Proposal** check for that level. This **Completes Proposal** check box should only ever be checked on the last step/level.

If you would like to discuss any of this in more detail, you can call our support line, and we can discuss in more detail if you like, or we can set up a Teams Meeting call with you for any training, or questions you would like, or have.

Did I answer all your questions, and did you understand my responses?

Does this help complete this support ticket, or do you have any more questions, or clarifications that are needed?

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike,



Thank you for speaking with me. I am in the process of updating the workflows to make sure I selected the correct Support option.



Under Request Change, please delete all but Revisions Requested.

Under Vote, please delete all but Approve, Support, Reviewed, and Implement:

1. Approve should move it forward and the Status should be Approved.
2. Please change Support to should move it forward, but keep it in review status or whatever, just not approved.
3. I do like that Reviewed doesn’t move anything forward or backward. Some Positions are just for notification, so I added Reviewed since I had to add an action.



I will send in a ticket about removing the Position of the one user that I cannot remove.



Thank you!



Craig Kutil

Las Positas College

(925) 424-1346

http://laspositascollege.edu/faculty/ckutil
http://www.laspositascollege.edu/lpcarticulation/

Craig,


1. The action Approve just moves the proposal forward or backwards, it doesn't change the status to Approved, unless you add that action at the last step/level and the user that takes that last action inputs a future date for when to go Active. If Approved action is in the middle levels, when that action is taken, it keeps the status In Review.
2. The Support action does the same as the Approve action.
3. The Reviewed action does move forward and backward.
These three actions, under **Vote**, all do the same thing. What makes them work differently is based off how you set up the step/position Required/Optional in position properties, and on the level properties.

I have moved this ticket into my queue to look into deactivating those actions that are not needed.

Hi Craig,
My name is Heather and I'm the developer working on this ticket.

For the header and footer issue, this is actually just a browser print setting that you can toggle on and off on your end.

Chrome or Edge:

- In the print menu popup, click the "More settings" dropdown to expand it, and uncheck the box next to "Headers and footers".
Firefox:

- In the print menu popup, click the "More settings" dropdown to expand it, and uncheck the box next to "Print headers and footers."

I will get working on the rest of the report fixes detailed in the PDF and will get this done ASAP.

Thank you,
~Heather Ludwig

- **Screenshot 4** shows what List B would look like on the report with the blank Course item removed and the Non Course Requirement added.

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any other questions or concerns while I work on the other report fixes.
